zh.lifeSummary: 當Hervé de LÉON I於大約1165年出生在法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾藍代維內時，他的父親Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé28歲，母親Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier16歲。他於1180年與Marguerite de ROHAN結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1203年8月4日在他的家鄉享年39歲，埋葬在法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾藍代維內。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hervé de LÉON I于大约1165年出生在法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾藍代維內时，他的父亲Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé28岁，母亲Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier16岁。他于1180年与Marguerite de ROHAN结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1203年8月4日在他的家乡享年39岁，埋葬在法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾藍代維內。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: LÉON
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Hervé
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally Breton. See Harvey .

en.lifeSummary: When Hervé de LÉON I was born about 1165, in Landévennec, Finistère, Brittany, France, his father, Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé, was 28 and his mother, Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier, was 16. He married Marguerite de ROHAN in 1180. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 4 August 1203, in his hometown, at the age of 39, and was buried in Landévennec, Finistère, Brittany, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hervé de LÉON I est né environ 1165, à Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, France, son père, Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Marguerite de ROHAN en 1180. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 4 août 1203, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hervé de LÉON I um 1165 in Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé 28 und seine Mutter Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier 16. Er hat 1180 Marguerite de ROHAN geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 4. August 1203 im Alter von 39 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hervé de LÉON I è nato il circa 1165 nel Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, il suo padre, Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Marguerite de ROHAN nel 1180. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 4 agosto 1203, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Hervé de LÉON Iが約1165年にフランスブレタグンフィニストeアランドeベネックで生まれた時，父Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insenséは28歳，母Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguierは16歳であった。1180年にMarguerite de ROHANと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1203年8月4日に故郷で39歳で死去し，フランスブレタグンフィニストeアランドeベネックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hervé de LÉON I(이)가 대략 1165년 프랑스 브레타그니 핑니스트e르 랜드e븐크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1180년 Marguerite de ROHAN(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1203년 8월 4일 그의 고향에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 브레타그니 핑니스트e르 랜드e븐크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hervé de LÉON I nasceu aproximadamente 1165, em Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Marguerite de ROHAN em 1180. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de agosto de 1203, em sua cidade natal, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hervé de LÉON I родился около 1165 г. в местности ландeвеннек, финистeр, бретагн, Франция, его отцу по имени Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite de ROHAN в 1180 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 4 августа 1203 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности ландeвеннек, финистeр, бретагн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hervé de LÉON I nació aproximadamente 1165, en Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Guiomarch de LÉON IV,  l'Insensé, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Nobilis de PENTHIÈVRE de Tréguier, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Marguerite de ROHAN en 1180. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 4 de agosto de 1203, en su pueblo, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Landévennec, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia.
